title: Why Your Animal-Based Diet Lacks Key Nutrients

chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The video begins with an introduction to the animal-based diet, presenting it as an evolutionarily consistent approach for humans that reflects their historical eating patterns as hunter-gatherers. The speaker emphasizes the nutrient-rich nature of animal-based foods while introducing the three main mistakes to avoid for better health.
chunks.transcript: here are the top three mistakes that you may be making on an animal-based diet you definitely want to avoid these things if you want to thrive eating this way an animal-based diet is what I believe is a very evolutionarily consistent diet for humans I believe this way of eating really mimics the way that humans have been eating for hundreds of thousands of years as hunter gatherers there are lots of ways for you to eat healthy but this is the blueprint that has worked best for me and I think it's a great starting point for people if they're doing an Elimination Diet and if you have autoimmune issues or if you just want to feel better you can always add more things to this diet but I think that these foods are the most nutrient-rich foods with the least amount of plant toxins I'm going to do a more detailed video about an animal-based diet The Ultimate Guide

chunks.title: Mistake 1: Insufficient Glycine and Collagen
chunks.summary: The first mistake highlighted is the lack of glycine and collagen in the diet, primarily from sources like bone broth. The speaker explains the importance of these amino acids in balancing muscle meat consumption and offers a method for making homemade bone broth to ensure proper intake.
chunks.transcript: I'll link to it in the show notes of this episode but what is an animal- based diet is organs I got some raw liver here I got some grass-fed meat it is fruit it is honey this is glyphosate free honey and it is raw dairy I've got some raw milk and some raw cheese there so what are the three mistakes that I think a lot of you guys might be making mistake number one not getting enough glycine not getting enough hydroxyproline not getting enough Proline this is not getting enough collagen so this is bone broth that I made myself collagen and bone broth is made up of predominantly three amino acids glycine Proline hydroxyproline there's a few others but these amino acids in collagen and bone broth balance the amino acids in stake when I see people doing an animal- based diet a lot of people will just eat muscle meat and they won't do the collagen they won't do bone broth so the amino acids in here especially Glycine and hydroxyproline are critical to balance the amino acids in here especially the branching amino acids in meat Lucine isol leucine and veine so do not make this mistake make your own bone broth it's easy you get bones knuckle bones I put them in a pressure cooker with water I set it to pressure cook and I'm done you can add salt if you want it's just bone broth made from Bones knuckle bones with a reverse osmosis water that's it all the good amino acids there balance what's in the steak so that's mistake number one don't miss out on that and as a caveat I'll tell you guys a lot of the collagen powders on the market are garbage they're made from hoof and hide cartilage or collagen and they're mostly sourced from South America Chile and Argentina and a lot of the stuff if you look at the analyses of these hydrolized collagen powders they're pretty high in heavy metals so I'm not a fan of those that's why I make my own bone broth so know the sourcing of your collagen just make your own bone broth that's the

chunks.title: Mistake 2: Inadequate Carbohydrate Intake
chunks.summary: In this segment, the focus shifts to the second mistake: not consuming enough carbohydrates, which the speaker argues are beneficial and even necessary for health. He shares his personal experience of improved health metrics after incorporating carbohydrates from fruits and honey into his diet.

chunks.title: Mistake 3: Neglecting Organ Meats
chunks.summary: The final mistake discussed is the common oversight of not including organ meats in an animal-based diet. The speaker emphasizes that organ meats are crucial for obtaining essential micronutrients, which muscle meat alone cannot provide, and suggests options for incorporating them into the diet.
chunks.transcript: sense of your Macros based on your weight and your activity level number three the third common mistake on an animal based diet is you're all just eating steak and you're not eating organs this is a big problem just like collagen balances the amino acids in meat organs balance the micronutrients in the meat if you look at meat muscle meat it's a good source of things like vitamin B12 vitamin B6 iron and then you look at organs like liver I've got some raw liver here it's a great source of minerals and nutrients that balance these out liver has folate not a lot of folate and meat liver has riboflavin not a lot of riboflavin and meat in fact it's really hard to get your RDA for riboflavin an essential nutrient for proper methylation without eating organs like liver and heart liver also is a good source of choline Biotin and copper to balance the zinc that you find in the meat there's not a lot of copper and meat and you don't want to get copper deficient you don't want to get zinc over copper so organs liver I do raw liver I do about a half an ounce to an ounce of raw liver per day and I do some heart every day a lot of you guys might not want to do fresh organs or it's hard to get I'll put a plug in here for hard and soil supplements I built this company guys I strongly believe in them it's grass-fed grass finished regeneratively raised organs and capsules always in glass because plastic is you can find that at heartand soil. I think fresh organs are best get your liver this way but if you won't do that this is going to change your life all right getting organs in your diet is the key thing that's number three that's it

chunks.title: Conclusion and Summary of Mistakes
chunks.summary: The video concludes with a recap of the three major mistakes people make on an animal-based diet. The speaker encourages viewers to be mindful of their dietary choices to enhance health and vitality.
chunks.transcript: guys those are the three biggest mistakes on animal based diet I hope
